title: Improve LoginPage styling and implement OTP backend functionality

requirements.text: Refine the LoginPage UI styling to improve visual hierarchy, spacing, and responsiveness while maintaining the existing light theme (white + blue color scheme), ensuring consistent padding, button styling, and mobile-friendly layout
acceptanceCriteria: LoginPage maintains white + blue color scheme
Form elements have consistent spacing and alignment
Buttons use consistent styling with hover states
Layout is responsive and works on mobile devices
Typography hierarchy is clear and readable

requirements.text: Implement backend method sendOTP that accepts a mobile number (Text, 10 digits) and generates a 6-digit OTP code, storing it temporarily with the mobile number and an expiration timestamp (5 minutes validity)

quotes: add the opt back end functionally

acceptanceCriteria: Backend accepts 10-digit mobile number as input
Generates random 6-digit numeric OTP
Stores OTP with mobile number and 5-minute expiration
Returns success response indicating OTP was generated
Validates mobile number format (exactly 10 digits)

requirements.text: Implement backend method verifyOTP that accepts mobile number and OTP code, validates the OTP against stored data, checks expiration, and returns authentication status with user ID if verification succeeds

quotes: add the opt back end functionally

acceptanceCriteria: Accepts mobile number and 6-digit OTP as input
Validates OTP matches the stored code for that mobile number
Checks OTP has not expired (within 5 minutes)
Returns success with user ID for valid OTP
Returns error for invalid or expired OTP
Clears OTP from storage after successful verification

requirements.text: Update useOTPAuth hook to integrate with backend sendOTP and verifyOTP methods, replacing mock localStorage logic with actual backend API calls for OTP generation and verification

quotes: add the opt back end functionally

acceptanceCriteria: useOTPAuth calls backend sendOTP method when sending OTP
useOTPAuth calls backend verifyOTP method during verification
Authentication state is updated based on backend verification response
Error handling for failed OTP operations is implemented
User session is established after successful OTP verification

requirements.text: Update LoginPage component to display loading states during OTP send and verification operations, showing appropriate feedback messages and disabling form inputs while backend requests are in progress

quotes: add the opt back end functionally

acceptanceCriteria: Send OTP button shows loading state while request is processing
Verify button shows loading state during OTP verification
Form inputs are disabled during active requests
Error messages are displayed for failed operations
Success feedback is shown before navigation to dashboard

constraints: Maintain single-actor backend architecture with all logic in backend/main.mo
Preserve existing useInternetIdentity.ts and useActor.ts files as read-only
Keep existing white + blue color scheme for LoginPage
Maintain current OTP flow: mobile entry → OTP verification → signup (for new users)
nonGoals: Integrating with external SMS gateway services
Implementing actual SMS delivery (mock/placeholder implementation is acceptable)
Changing authentication flow or adding alternative login methods
Modifying other pages beyond LoginPage styling improvements
